pr.title: Further splitting of MSL C related functions and sysdolphin
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly organization PR that split a monolithic asm/text_2.s region into named MSL C library assembly files, moved remaining unsplit sysdolphin material into asm/text_3.s, began splitting sysdolphin functions into domain files, and corrected an objalloc split where the first function had actually belonged at the end of another file. The main value was improving file/module boundaries and linker-script ordering while preserving raw assembly matches.
what_changed: Removed most of the old monolithic content from asm/text_2.s, with the changed-file summary showing 6852 deleted lines and only 31 added lines.
Added new MSL C-related assembly files including abort_exit.s, ansi_fp.s, buffer_io.s, direct_io.s, cstring.s, ctype.s, mem_funcs.s, memory.s, and critical_regions.gamecube.s.
Moved remaining unsplit sysdolphin code into asm/text_3.s, which was added with 1731 lines according to the PR file list.
Began splitting sysdolphin code into named files such as dobj.s, robj.s, tobj.s, quatlib.s, spline.s, displayfunc.s, and objalloc.s.
Updated ldscript.lcf to account for the new assembly-file boundaries and preserve the expected link order.
Corrected the objalloc split after discovering the first objalloc function was actually located at the end of another file.
The diff excerpt shows extracted raw assembly functions retaining addresses and symbols such as exit in asm/abort_exit.s, __num2dec_internal in asm/ansi_fp.s, and __flush_buffer in asm/buffer_io.s.
smart_moves: Used library/module names for split files instead of leaving large anonymous text blobs, making future decompilation work more searchable and reviewable.
Separated known MSL C runtime functions from unsplit sysdolphin code, reducing ambiguity between compiler/runtime library code and game/engine code.
Kept unsplit residue in text_3.s rather than forcing uncertain names or boundaries, which preserved correctness while still improving organization.
Adjusted the linker script together with the assembly splits, which is essential when file boundaries change but the final linked binary layout must remain stable.
Fixed a mistaken objalloc boundary by moving the first function out of objalloc when evidence showed it belonged at the end of another file.
decomp_lessons: When splitting monolithic assembly, validate function boundaries against neighboring files; the objalloc note shows that a module's apparent first function may actually be tail content from the previous module.
For library/runtime code, file names should track known CodeWarrior/MSL module names when available, such as abort_exit, ansi_fp, buffer_io, direct_io, cstring, ctype, mem_funcs, and memory.
It is acceptable to create an intermediate unsplit holding file for a subsystem, such as text_3.s for sysdolphin, while progressively extracting better-understood modules.
Every assembly split that changes object boundaries must be mirrored in ldscript.lcf so link order and section layout remain consistent.
Raw assembly migration should retain global labels and address comments during early organization work so later C matching can use stable anchors.
naming_conventions: MSL C runtime assembly files were named after recognizable library components: abort_exit.s, ansi_fp.s, buffer_io.s, direct_io.s, cstring.s, ctype.s, mem_funcs.s, memory.s, and critical_regions.gamecube.s.
sysdolphin-related files used short subsystem names already visible in the project context: dobj.s, robj.s, tobj.s, objalloc.s, quatlib.s, and spline.s.
Platform-specific runtime code used a dotted suffix in critical_regions.gamecube.s.

Known external/library symbols kept their conventional leading underscores where present, including __num2dec_internal, __flush_buffer, __destroy_global_chain, __kill_critical_regions, and _ExitProcess.
Unknown local labels remained address-derived lbl_XXXXXXXX names, indicating the PR did not invent semantic names where evidence was lacking.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split assembly by contiguous object/module regions while preserving original instruction comments and addresses.
Kept .include "macros.inc" and .section .text declarations in each newly extracted assembly file.
Preserved .global declarations for known entry points such as exit, __num2dec_internal, and __flush_buffer.
Used ldscript.lcf updates as the coordination point for newly split asm files, a required tactic to keep binary layout stable after moving code between files.
Left uncertain sysdolphin material in an unsplit text_3.s bucket rather than over-splitting without confidence.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review according to the metadata, but no review-comment excerpt or actionable reviewer feedback was included.

The only explicit rationale in the human text was the author summary: split text_2 into MSL C functions, move the other half to text_3 for unsplit sysdolphin functions, begin splitting those, and fix the objalloc split.
searchable_terms: Further splitting of MSL C related functions and sysdolphin
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert the newly split MSL C assembly files into C.
Find later PRs that continue splitting asm/text_3.s sysdolphin functions.
Search for objalloc boundary fixes or comments explaining which function was moved out of objalloc.
Inspect ldscript.lcf history around PR #20 to confirm exact object ordering introduced by the split.
Look for subsequent renames of dobj, robj, tobj, quatlib, and spline files into C source/header structure.
